description: Adversarial invariant for the manual dialysis-machine and medical-linear-accelerator audit: therapeutic device nodes must not depend on hospital IT or diagnostic-lab proxies when source-backed method edges exist.

checks.reason: Dialysis machines are therapeutic renal-support devices rather than diagnostic laboratory methods.
Hospital information systems are not part of dialysis-machine architecture or the 1943 artificial-kidney method.
The early selective membrane foundation should remain anchored to Graham's 1861 dialysis membrane work rather than a generated 1945 placeholder.
Pump and flow architecture supports practical dialysis-machine scaling without being the original 1943 gravity-fed separation method.
Clinical medicine is the therapeutic care context for dialysis machines.
Dialysis machines should stay anchored to Kolff's first patient treatment with the rotating-drum artificial kidney.
Hospital information systems are not medical-linear-accelerator hardware or treatment-beam prerequisites.
A medical linac is a clinical implementation of linear particle-accelerator hardware.
Hospital radiotherapy/radiology departments are deployment context rather than the accelerator component itself.
Medical linacs should stay anchored to the 1953 first therapy-linac patient at Hammersmith Hospital.
